issues_found: PAGE_TYPE is listing_page — CORE RULE 5 mandates rejection for listing pages
Source is a chronological index/listing page containing ~100+ separate press releases, not a single coherent source
Cannot be treated as a single archiveable research note
Individual 2014 FOMC statements and releases embedded in this index would be individually relevant but must be accessed as separate sources
Suggested_status incorrectly set to 'review_queue' when clear rejection criteria apply per CORE RULE 5
human_review_reason: Record correctly identifies the source as a non-archivable listing page. No ambiguity exists — CORE RULE 5 explicitly requires rejection for listing_page type. The record's own analysis supports rejection; no human review needed to confirm.

corrected_fields.summary: This is a chronological archive listing page of all Federal Reserve press releases from calendar year 2014, containing dozens of unrelated items spanning monetary policy statements, regulatory actions, enforcement actions, banking applications, and administrative announcements. The page is not a single coherent source but rather an index of approximately 100+ separate press releases. Per CORE RULE 5, listing pages are non-archiveable.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This source is a navigational archive page that aggregates 2014 Federal Reserve communications. Individual press releases from this page (such as FOMC statements on 12/17/2014, 10/29/2014, and the FOMC projections release) would be individually relevant and archiveable, but the listing page itself is not a direct source of market structure or macro catalyst content and must be rejected per CORE RULE 5.
